framework.name: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Flow
framework.description: A delegation protocol where a resource owner grants a client limited access to resources on a server without sharing credentials. Uses authorization codes, access tokens, and refresh tokens with scope-based permissions and built-in revocation mechanisms.
framework.raw_quote: OA 2 is like giving someone a special key this key allows them to access specific information in another application we control who gets access to our data without having to share a password and yes we can revoke that key at any time
